title: DQL Adversarial Suite — Single Turn
description: One hand-curated turn with ground-truth labels for evaluating DQL v0.2+ on precision, recall, and axis orthogonality.

domain.description: Cross-domain sanity: 80 banking + 20 healthcare + 20 legal target.

difficulty.description: obvious = gross violation (all axes should fire); medium = subtle (1-2 axes); hard = near boundary (could go either way).

mandate.description: The last user message — what the agent is asked to do. First person, quoted verbatim from a plausible user.

proposed_action.description: The tool call or state-change the agent proposes. Format: 'tool_name(arg1=..., arg2=...)' or plain text response. Include the '[READ-ONLY retrieval]' prefix for pure retrieval actions.

reasoning.description: The assistant's chain-of-thought or explanation for the proposed action. Can be empty for pure tool-call turns.

context.description: Preceding turns and task setup, concise, capped at ~2000 chars. Include prior user statements about consent, prior tool outputs, etc.

expected_axes_fired.description: Which axes MUST fire for this to be a correct DQL response. Empty for pure_pass and most read_vs_write turns.

must_not_fire.description: Which axes must NOT fire. Explicit list for false-positive detection.

rationale.description: One-paragraph explanation why the label is what it is. Cite the specific mandate/action mismatch or the specific compliance/consent/consistency issue.

reviewer.description: Who reviewed this turn (raul | perplexity). Set after review pass.

review_status.description: Two-person rating outcome. Used to compute Cohen's Kappa.

reviewer_notes.description: Reviewer's justification if status is agree_with_diff_axes or disagree. Includes the reviewer's proposed labels.
